title: mentha longifolia subsp. serrulata
intro: Mentha longifolia subsp. serrulata, also known as Hoary Mint or Woolly Mint, is a subspecies of the Mentha longifolia plant, a type of mint belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, valued for its medicinal properties due to its high concentration of volatile oils, particularly menthone and menthyl acetate. The plant has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems, with its essential oils exhibiting antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, contributing to its potential health benefits in reducing stress, improving sleep, and alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. Hoary Mint can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are commonly used in aromatherapy and traditional medicine.
